scenes.scene_description: The Wedding Chapel, continued from last season. Chandler and Monica are about to get married.
Suddenly the doors burst open, and ROSS AND RACHEL COME OUT ARM-IN-ARM!!!!! And Rachel's carrying a bouquet!!! THEY GOT MARRIED!!!!
They storm out into the street.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Whoa!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Come on Pheebs! Hurry! Hurry! Hurry! Hurry!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay! Okay! Okay!

scenes.scene_description: She goes one way; he goes the other. The camera pans back to Chandler and Monica, and needless to say, they're standing there dumbstruck.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh my God!! Is everybody getting married?!!

scenes.scene_description: Chandler and Monica are stunned again.

lines.speaker: Attendant
lines.text: N-No running in the chapel!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey! Don’t you give me any of your—Hey!
lines.stage_direction: to her Sees Chandler and Monica standing there.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What are you guys doing here?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ross and Rachel left us a message saying they were getting married! Isn’t that why you guys are here?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes! Well that-yes.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Why else would we be here?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well! What happened?! Did we miss it?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: We actually missed it.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, maybe you wouldn’t have had you run in the chapel !

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: This is insane!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What’s the big deal, y’know? It’s not like it’s a real marriage.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, if you get married in Vegas, you’re only married in Vegas.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What are you talking about? If you get married in Vegas you’re married everywhere.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Really?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God!—Eh! Well…

scenes.scene_description: Phoebe and Joey run back out and head towards the street.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohhh!
lines.stage_direction: Looks in the mirror and sees that she still has her mustache and groans.

scenes.scene_description: Rachel’s hotel room, she’s waking up with a horrendous hangover.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Why are we in bed together?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don’t know. Do-do you have any clothes on?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Really?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No! But we-we didn’t have…sex-uh, did we? I mean, I don’t remember much about last night, it was such a blur.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh! I remember laughing! I laughed a lot.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And we didn’t have sex.

scenes.scene_description: Suddenly, there’s movement beside her, startling her. It’s Ross! He wakes up and they both start screaming in terror. Then they both grab their heads having aggravated their hangovers.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh, I mean, we were really drunk. I’m just glad we didn’t do anything stupid.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Tell me about it.
lines.stage_direction: getting up He sits up on the edge of the bed and has "Just Married" written on his back.

scenes.scene_description: Rachel agrees with him and starts to get out of bed.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Mornin’ Pheebs. Well, my movie has officially been canceled.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh Joey, I’m so sorry. You want some of my breakfast?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Nah, I’m too depressed to eat. I’ll probably eat in like 5 minutes. So I guess I’ll just fly home with you guys, what time’s your flight?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What about my cab?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don’t need that anymore.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, Joey! You borrowed my cab; you have to drive it back.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don’t want to drive all the way back by myself, I get so lonely. Oh—ooh! How about you come with me?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I don’t know, it’s such a long trip.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: It’ll be great! We-we could talk, and play games! Huh? This could be our chance to like renew our friendship.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Are you asking me to have a frenaissance?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Sure?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right. Although I don’t think we need one, I never stopped loving you.

scenes.scene_description: The breakfast buffet, Phoebe is already sitting at the table as Joey enters.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So, has anyone talked to Dr. and Mrs. Geller yet?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Um-hmm, yeah. They left me a message; they should be here any minute.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Where is the waitress?! I’m starving!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It’s a buffet man.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, here’s where I win all my money back!
lines.stage_direction: Gets up and heads for the buffet table with Chandler in tow.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Listen, I gotta talk to you.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Sure! What’s up?
lines.stage_direction: He grabs a plate and proceeds to load it with a huge pile of scrambled eggs. Chandler just stares at him and Joey reluctantly gives him a spoonful.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Monica and I almost got married last night.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh my God! That’s huge! Wait a minute, why come I wasn’t invited? And who was going to be your best man? Don’t say, "Ross." Do not say, "Ross."

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Look, I just don’t think Monica and I are ready to get married yet! Y’know? I mean, I love her and everything but seeing Ross and Rachel coming out of that chapel was like a, like a wake-up call that Monica and I are moving so fast. Y’know? And, how do I tell her without crushing her?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh! Tell her she’s not marriage material.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Girls say it to me all the time! And believe me, if she’s anything like me, she’s just gonna be relieved.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: How do I tell Chandler that it’s too soon. It’s gonna break his heart, he’s not gonna think that I don’t love him anymore.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well you don’t.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes I do!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Good! Good! I was just testing you.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh hi! Hi! Y’know, we were just talking about bacon.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, we were talking about tennis. Tennis is more believable.

scenes.scene_description: Chandler and Monica enter.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey!

scenes.scene_description: Ross and Rachel enter.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Are we gonna talk about what you guys did last night? Or…

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don’t know. What do you mean last night? Nothing, nothing uh, happened last night.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uh-huh! Ross invited us all to watch.

scenes.scene_description: They both sit down and Rachel pours them both some coffee. They’re acting like nothing’s happened and everyone is just staring at them.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Rach! We weren’t gonna miss our friends getting married!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Who got married?!
lines.stage_direction: gasps Ross is as surprised to hear this as she is.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You did.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?! Hello! We didn’t get married.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, we didn’t get married! That’s ridiculous!

scenes.scene_description: Rachel turns to Ross stunned.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: We-we-we—I remember being in a chapel.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I—They would not let us get married when we were that drunk!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: They let you get married when you’re drunk! Most people who get married in Vegas are drunk!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hell, I’m drunk right now! What? I can’t have a mimosa with breakfast?! I’m on vacation!
lines.stage_direction: They all turn and look at her.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What are you guys gonna do?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I guess we just find a divorce lawyer?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I think, I think, Ross already has one. Now, this one’s free, right? Because you paid for the first two, so the third one’s free.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Laugh it up, but the joke’s on you. Because we don’t need to get divorced, okay? We we’re just gonna get an annulment.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: An annulment? Ross! I don’t think surgery’s the answer here.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh-oh, that’s your thing.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You’re thing. You’re thing. Y’know? You’re the guy who gets divorced.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Oh yeah!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No-no, that’s-that’s not my thing! I do not love getting divorced!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yes you do! This is your third divorce! You love divorce so much you’re probably gonna marry it! Then it won’t work out and you’re gonna have to divorce it, divorcing guy. I’m so drunk.

scenes.scene_description: They turn to look at each other and suddenly remember that they did in fact get married.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So, what do you think we should do?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I don’t know. But I-I-I know I love you!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know I love you!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So where are we on the whole going back to the place where they have all the marriages thing? I love you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That’s a good question. Look umm, last night we let the dice decide. Maybe we should leave it up to fate again. I love you!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, we don’t get married unless there’s a sign! Okay, so say uh, say you roll another eight then there’s a definite sign that we should get married.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, eight we get married, but 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 9, 10, 11, 12 we don’t get married.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Sounds great.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Croupler
lines.text: Coming in, we got a shooter! Money please.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ready?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ready!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Come on eight.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, yes eight.

scenes.scene_description: The casino floor, Chandler and Monica are walking through it.

lines.speaker: Croupler
lines.text: Eight! Easy eight.
lines.stage_direction: She rolls a 3 and a 5 and they’re stunned.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wow! I can’t believe I actually rolled an eight.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That was so unlikely. Well, let’s get married! I guess.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wait a minute. That wasn’t a hard eight! Last night I rolled a hard eight.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That’s right! It was the wrong kind of eight, no wedding! Damnit!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I wanted it so bad! Wanna go pack?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah. We’re doing the right thing, right?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ohh, of course we are! We left it up to fate. If we were supposed to get married there would be a clear-cut sign.
lines.stage_direction: They walk up to the elevators. Pushes the elevator button. The elevator door opens to a priest reading from a bible with Chandler and Monica standing side-by-side holding each other’s hands.

scenes.scene_description: Monica rolls the dice.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, you have 19 questions left. Use them wisely. Come on Joey! You can’t win if you don’t ask any QUESTIONS !!!
lines.stage_direction: Joey groans. sees that he’s asleep

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, you promised me a fun road trip! We’ve been on the road six hours and you’ve been asleep for five and a half! We are switching at the next rest stop and you are going to drive all the way back! That will be your punishment, you greedy sleeper!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right. All right.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah! And until then you are going to sing to me because the radio’s broken and you are selfish but have a nice voice.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Really? I don’t…

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Sing!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Man, this is a long drive! Are my eyes open? No!
lines.stage_direction: to himself He opens his eyes and sees a hitchhiker.

lines.speaker: Hitchhiker
lines.text: Morning!

scenes.scene_description: Phoebe’s cab, Phoebe and Joey are driving back. Phoebe is driving while Joey is sleeping.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey, hubby!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah. Yeah, actually um, I wanted to talk to you about that whole annulment thing?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh-huh.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I’m not going to do that.

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, Rachel is on the couch as Ross enters.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay! So, we’ll just stay married.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes, exactly!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And I will make everyone call me Mrs. Geller!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wow! This is so amazing. I uh, I really thought I’d have to talk you into this more.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, see now I’m scared because I don’t actually think you’re kidding.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I’m-I’m not kidding. Look I-I, I can’t have three failed marriages. I can’t. Okay? I-I am not gonna be that guy!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What-wh-what so we’ll just stay married forever?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, look, how is this gonna affect you? Really? I mean you fill some form out once and a while and instead of checking the box that says Ms you check the box that says Mrs.! It’s right next to it!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh, okay, I’m sorry. You’re right. Y’know what? We absolutely can stay married, because I was under the impression that the boxes were far away from each other. All right, look, just please, take a moment here and think about what you’re asking of me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I’m asking you to do me a favor.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You are asking me to be your wife!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And as my wife I think you should grant me this favor.

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, continued from earlier. Rachel starts laughing.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That kid really kicked me hard on the plane.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well you did pull his hair.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: He took my snack!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I’m not getting into this again!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay! Oh God, y’know what? It’s really bad.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I told you not to walk. Here. There. Okay.
lines.stage_direction: Picks her up. He opens the door, carries her across the threshold, stops, backs out, and lets go of Monica who is only holding on by his neck.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: This doesn’t mean anything, does it?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay.

scenes.scene_description: The hallway, Chandler is helping Monica to the door.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: How could you pick up a hitchhiker?! He could be a rape—, a rapist or a killer or something!
lines.stage_direction: To Joey She holds her hand in front of the hitchhiker’s face

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Don’t you think I asked him that before he got in?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Y’know what? I’m not talking to you! You go back to sleep! And you, are you a rapist?!

lines.speaker: Hitchhiker
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Do you like car games?

lines.speaker: Hitchhiker
lines.text: Yeah, y’know the license plate game?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I love the license plate game!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ooh, I’ll play! I’ll play!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No-No! You need your sleep. Night-night! Shh!

scenes.scene_description: Phoebe’s cab, she’s driving, Joey’s in the back seat, and the hitchhiker is riding up front with Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Listen, I know you wanted to talk to me, but I have an idea that may make you want to stay married. We register, and you get to keep all the presents!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No! Ross, come on! No! Listen, look I thought a lot about how to tell you this and the bottom line, Ross, is we can not stay married.
lines.stage_direction: thinks about it for a second

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I don’t know if it’s true.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh b-b-but it is!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, okay, y’know what this is? This is a difference of opinion. And when that happens in a marriage...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh Ross, come on! This is not, this is not a marriage!! This is the world’s worst hangover! Ross, listen, if you do not get this annulment, I will!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right. All right, I’ll do it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Thank you. Hey-hey umm, uh, is there, is there any such thing as an annulment shower?

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's, Ross is entering.
Ross turns and leaves.

lines.speaker: Hitchhiker
lines.text: Wait! Wait! There is the train station!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, okay.

lines.speaker: Hitchhiker
lines.text: This is where I get off. Well, I have your address and phone number.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: And I have your name and the fact that you’re a drifter, so the ball’s pretty much in your court.

lines.speaker: Hitchhiker
lines.text: All right, see ya Pheebs.
lines.stage_direction: Gets out and Phoebe drives away.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Come on Pheebs! I can’t take this anymore! Let-let me make it up to you. Huh? Ground control to Major Tom. Commencing countdown, engines on. Take your protein pills and put your helmet on !

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Stop it! Stop it no! That’s not fair! Y’know I can’t resist that beautiful voice!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Pheebs, I am so sorry! I know I promised you a fun road trip with your friend and I didn’t deliver. But-but-but now I know that you think being awake is an important part of friendship! So, so, so I will strive to-to stay awake for as long as I know you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You can still sleep at night and stuff.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, thank you. So, can we play 20 Questions now? I’ve got a really good one! I’ve been thinking about it since Kansas.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. Is it a kind of hot sandwich?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Is it a meatball sub?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That is incredible! You are the master!

scenes.scene_description: Phoebe’s cab, it’s the same arrangement as before.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Huh, that’s funny. You look like you’re gonna be the…

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, don’t say it! Don’t even think it!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right. Okay Chandler, enjoy your handful.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right, should we just, should we just get married? Y’know? I mean should we just do it? All the signs are telling us to do it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I’m sick of the signs! It’s too fast, I’m happy the way things are!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Me too!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I don’t want things to change! Do you?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right then, then nothing changes! Everything is great! Everything stays the same! And you go unpack because it’s been three days and it’s driving me insane!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Jeez, relax! It’s not like we’re mar-ah-ah!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y’know I was thinking, what if I uh, unpack here?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Then all your stuff would be here.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, what if all my stuff was here?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Then you’d be going back and forth all the time, I mean it doesn’t make any sense.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay. What if we lived together and you understand what I’m saying?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Live together? There have been no signs for that.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Me asking is kind of a sign.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay!!!!!

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's, Chandler and Monica are there and Rachel is arranging a bouquet of flowers, pricks her finger on a thorn, throws the bouquet over her head, and those you who are quicker than some already know that Monica is the one who catches it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes! Okay! Okay! Wait-wait-wait!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Here’s your key

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh thanks.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Here’s your key. All right, you have to christen it! Now, go out and come back in!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: The door hasn’t been locked in five years, but okay! Ready?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ready!

scenes.scene_description: And the crowd goes wild! Well, at least the live studio audience.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, a little problem. The key broke in the lock and I can’t get in!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wait! Oh my God! I can’t get out!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: This is not a sign!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, it’s not a sign! It’s a very old key!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It’s an old key!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God it’s old!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I love you!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I love you!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Are you hugging the door right now.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah-yeah, me neither.

scenes.scene_description: He tries, but something happens.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey, so did everything go all right with the annulment?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, yeah, no problems. It’s all taken care of.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross, thank you. Hey, do you guys wanna go see a movie?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh yeah, why not?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Pheebs?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No thanks, I’ve already seen one.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, umm, I’m gonna get my sweater.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay. You uh, you wanna hear something weird?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Always.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I didn’t get the annulment.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: We’re still married! Don’t tell Rachel. See you later.
lines.stage_direction: He gets up leaving Phoebe in shock.

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, Rachel and Phoebe are on the couch as Ross enters.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What are you doing?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: The key’s stuck in the lock.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I can fix it. Hold on. Look out. Look out.
lines.stage_direction: He goes and gets a screwdriver from his apartment Pries at the door a little bit.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It still doesn’t work.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I’m not finished.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh.

scenes.scene_description: The hallway, Joey is coming up the stairs and sees Chandler trying to open the lock.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Nice job Joe! You’re quite the craftsmen.

scenes.scene_description: Joey goes back into the apartment, runs back into the hallway, throws his shoulder against the door, and knocks it down off it’s hinges.
Joey pats him on the stomach and heads to bed.

title: The One Where Ross Hugs Rachel

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey, so did everything go okay with the annulment?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, yeah, no problems. It’s all taken care of.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross, thank you. Hey, do you guys wanna go see a movie?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh yeah, why not?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, umm, I’m gonna get my sweater.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay. You uh, you wanna hear something weird?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Always.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I didn’t get the annulment.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: We’re still married! Don’t tell Rachel. See you later.
lines.stage_direction: He gets up leaving Phoebe in shock.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, do you have any gum?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh? Yeah! Sure!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wait! Wait! Hi! Listen, Ross can I just talk to you for just a second?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh but Phoebe, we’re gonna be late for the movie.

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, it’s the same scene from the end of last week’s show. Phoebe and Rachel are sitting on the couch as Ross enters.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, there’s a cab! Taxi!! Good timing, my God, huh? Here you go.
lines.stage_direction: The cab stops and she opens the door. Pushes Rachel in and closes the door.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, we’re, we’re actually just gonna walk ‘cause it’s right up there at the Angelica.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, the Angelica!! Go! Go! You didn’t get the annulment?!!
lines.stage_direction: She bangs on the cab’s roof and it pulls away. To Ross

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I know.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ross?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, I tried! But when I got to my lawyer’s office all I could hear was, "Three divorces. Three divorces!" Look, I just don’t want my tombstone to read, Ross Gellar, Three Divorces.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Don’t be worried about that! Your tombstone can say whatever you want it to say! It could say, Ross Geller, Good at Marriage! Y’know? Mine’s gonna say Phoebe Buffay, Buried Alive.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look, all I know is I-I can’t have another failed marriage!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So okay what? You’re gonna be married to a girl who doesn’t even know about it?!—Op, woman ! Sorry.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, okay so, I don’t have it all worked out quite yet. Just don’t say anything to Rachel, please?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Rachel is one of my closest friends. Although, being the only one who knows anything about this does makes me feel special. Okay!

scenes.scene_description: A cab pulls up.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, stop-stop! Phoebe?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey Rach!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What was that?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Sorry, mix up. Hey, how was the movie?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I haven’t seen it yet!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well then you’d better hurry! The Angelica! Go! Go!
lines.stage_direction: Bangs on the roof again and off they go.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Noooooooo!!

scenes.scene_description: Rachel’s cab backs up.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y’know when we move in together, can I get a gumball machine?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Of course! Joey wouldn’t let you have one?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No. When it comes to sweets, he’s surprisingly strict.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, have you figured out a way to tell him you’re moving out?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, no, I keep trying, y’know? I can get out, "Joey, I have too…" but then I lose my nerve and I always finish with, "…go to the bathroom." He may think I’m sick.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Y’know, I really have to tell Rachel, but I… We just have to get it over with! Y’know, the next time we see them we’re just gonna tell them. Okay? That’s it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, so that’s this is gonna work now? You’re just gonna order me around all the time?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Pretty much.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey Monica!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey man, you feeling any better?
lines.stage_direction: To Chandler Chandler answers him with some guttural sounds that only he can make and that no human can transcribe.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Joey, we have something to tell you.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh my God! You’re pregnant!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No-o-o! No? No-o-o! Look Joey, here’s the thing, Monica and I have decided to live together, here. So, I’m gonna be moving out man.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wow! Well, uh… Hey! I’m really happy for you guys! Congratulations! See you later.
lines.stage_direction: Tearing up Kisses Monica on the cheek Starts for the door on the verge of tears as Monica stops him.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wait! Joey! Joey! Are you okay?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, I gotta go! I got an acting job. Like you’d believe that. This sucks!
lines.stage_direction: Turns towards the door, pauses, and turns back.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Look, I-I’m just gonna be right across the hall and I promise you, the minute Monica and I break up I’m moving right back in with you!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay! Look-look-look, uh, if you’re gonna be moving in with him I feel it’s my responsibility to tell you the truth about him! Okay? He’s a terrible roommate! Terrible! He uh, forgets to umm… Oh-oh he always, he always umm—Oh, who am I kidding! He’s the best roommate ever!

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's, Chandler and Monica are cuddling on the couch.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey Pheebs!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, have you seen Rachel?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, why?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I have some pretty exciting news!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You’re pregnant!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No! Chandler and I are moving in together!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh! That’s good! Wow! But now if you were pregnant, what would you name it? Hint: Phoebe.

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, Phoebe is on the couch as Monica enters.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey! Hi!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What’s up?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well umm, Chandler and I are moving in together.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh my God. Ohh, my little sister and my best friend…shaking up. Oh, that’s great. That’s great.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Guys, I’m happy too.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, come here!
lines.stage_direction: Phoebe joins them in the hug.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wow! Big day huh? People moving in, people getting annulled…

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, I gotta go find Rachel but umm, if you guys see her could you please try to give her some really bad news so that mine doesn’t seem so bad?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Bye.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hmm, something bad to tell Rachel… Bad news for Rachel, what could that be?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Could ya just, could ya just lay off, please? All right? My life is an embarrassment! I should go live under somebody’s stairs!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ross, it’s not that big a deal! So you’ll been divorced three times, you’ll still have a life, you’ll go on dates…

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No! No, I won’t! I’ll be at the bottom of the dating barrel now. The only guys below me will be Four Divorce Guy uh, Murderer Guy, and-and, Geologists.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ross, you’re being ridiculous! Okay? You are cute and smart and sweet and that is much more important than three stupid divorces!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh yeah? Have you ever dated anyone who has been divorced three times?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Y’know that’s really fair. Y’know? Most guys who have been divorced three times are like 60. Ross, nobody cares about this except you! This-this embarrassment thing is all in your head! Here, I’ll show you! Come here.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?! Why?

scenes.scene_description: Ross returns from getting some coffee.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hi! Hi! Listen, my friend Ross is about to be divorced for the third time, but wouldn’t you date him?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And if you wait right here, I’ll go get Ross.
lines.stage_direction: Phoebe grabs his arm and prevents him from escaping.

scenes.scene_description: She grabs his arm and drags him over to a table where three beautiful women are sitting. Now, I’m going to go out on a limb and say that their names are Stephanie, Karin, and Meg. Okay, so I looked at the credits. Of course, only Meg is named later on, so I’ll have to guess who is Stephanie and who is Karin. But, we’ll cross that bridge when we come to it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ugh, I thought you were Rachel!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What gave me away?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I just tell her, I have to get it over with. I told Ross and Phoebe and she’s the only one left!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, so that’s it, everybody knows! It’s official, we’re moving in together. No turning back. Are ya scared? Are ya?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Wow that was my scariest voice! You’re very brave.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's, Monica is pacing anxiously waiting for Rachel.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ugh, the worse day! Y’know, you think you’re making progress at work and then your boss calls you Raquel.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey listen, for the first four years of my work everybody called me Sha-la-lap. Seriously.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I believe you. So, it was right in the middle of a staff meeting so of course no one else wants to correct her so everyone else is calling me Raquel! By the end of the day, the mailroom guys were calling me Rocky!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I-I still think you’re very-very nice and very pretty…

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All yours babe.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Have a seat. Okay, listen umm, Chandler and I are going to live together, here.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God! That’s so great! I’m so happy for you guys!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Really?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And that was so sweet of you to ask! Oh my God, the three of us are gonna have such a good time living together!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes, we are.
lines.stage_direction: Chandler is frantically trying to get Monica to correct her.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And Chandler, you’re gonna have to watch those long showers you take in the morning because you know Raquel can’t be late.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Rach…

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, he’s gonna work on that.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh! This is so exciting! Oh God… Come and knock on my door …

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We’ll be waiting for you …

scenes.scene_description: They hug as Rachel enters.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: …once you know the stories, it’s not that bad. First marriage, wife’s hidden sexuality, not my fault. Second marriage, said the wrong name at the altar, a little my fault. Third marriage, well they really shouldn’t allow you to get married when you’re that drunk and have writing all over your face, Nevada’s fault.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, so what do you think ladies? Who wouldn’t be interested? Who wouldn’t want to date him?

lines.speaker: Stephanie
lines.text: Well the divorces don’t bother me, I’d date him. But, not while he’s still married.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, what about you? Wouldn’t you want a date?

lines.speaker: Karin
lines.text: Actually, I’m dating at all anymore. See, I figured out that I was only dating guys that were like y’know bad for me, so until I work that out…

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Whatever! What about you Meg?

lines.speaker: Meg
lines.text: Well, I don’t care about the divorces either, but I wouldn’t date him. It’s just that he’s obviously still in love with this Rachel girl.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: She said, "He’s obviously still in love with this Rachel girl."
lines.stage_direction: leaning to him He glares at her.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: This is crazy! I mean, yes-yes Rachel is my good friend and I-I have loved her in the past, but now , she is just my wife! Phoebe, will you-will you help me out here?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, I thought you loved her when you-when you married her.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: We were drunk! I would’ve married uh, Joey with that amount of alcohol!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey! You could do a lot worse than Joey Tribbiani!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look, I’m sorry, but you guys are wrong. I just don’t want to be divorced three times.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yes, and now he’s using this three divorces reason because he wants to stay married to her because he loves her. I must say, "Well done!" Bravo Meg.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay! Fine! Fine! If-if this is what you think, forget about the whole three divorce thing! Okay, I-I’ll go to my lawyer’s office right now and get this marriage annulled! Okay?! Because she means nothing to me! Noth——Nothing!!
lines.stage_direction: getting up leans down to Meg’s ear

lines.speaker: Meg
lines.text: Okay now I wouldn’t date you because you seem a little creepy.

lines.speaker: Karin
lines.text: I am so attracted to him right now.

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, Phoebe is hosting an impromptu roundtable discussion with Stephanie, Karin, and Meg about Ross’s three divorces.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, when are we gonna tell Rachel what is actually gonna happen?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Soon! I-I just couldn’t before. You saw how upset Joey got! I couldn’t do that to her, she’s my best friend!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, Joey’s my best friend.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I’m not your best friend?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You just said… Of course you’re my best friend. Would you please tell Rachel though?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, all right, at least I’m prepared.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay. Oh yes.
lines.stage_direction: She grabs a tin of freshly baked cookies He reaches for one.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey-hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: These aren’t for you! Are you upset?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I am now!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey roomie!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, bye!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Rach, there’s something uh, important I have to tell you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Are you pregnant?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No! But, I’m throwing this shirt away! I think there was a little misunderstanding before.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Um-hmm.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Umm, when I said that uh, that Chandler and I wanted to umm, live together we meant alone together.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God! Oh, that’s funny, I can’t believe I did that.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh no sweetie, no! This is my fault, I wasn’t clear! I’m really sorry. And listen, you take as much time as you need to move out okay? There’s absolutely no rush.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.
lines.stage_direction: Sits back and resumes her reading.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay. Don’t you want a cookie?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Sure? Thanks.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Maybe, do you need a tissue?

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's, Chandler and Monica are still debating about how to tell Rachel that Chandler’s moving in and she’s moving out.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Monica, where did you get these?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I made them!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ooh, good God, they’re so yummy!
lines.stage_direction: She re-ingests the previously expelled cookie matter from the tissue.

scenes.scene_description: Rachel mumbles uh-huh and spits the half-eaten cookie out onto the tissue.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: God, it’s gonna so weird like when I come home and you’re not here. Y’know? No more Joey and Chan’s. No more J and C’s. "You wanna go over to Joey and Chandler’s?" "Can’t, its not there."

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Look, I’m just gonna be across the hall, we can still do all the same stuff.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah but we won’t be able to like get up in the middle of the night and have those long talks about our feelings and the future.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Not once did we do that.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So I, I told Rachel it was just gonna be the two of us.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh yeah? Well, how’d she take it?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Really well. Yeah. Surprisingly well. Yeah, she didn’t cry. She wasn’t angry or sad.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And you’re upset because you didn’t make your best friend cry?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I mean, all I’m asking for is just a little emotion! Is that too much to ask after six years?! I mean what? Are-are-are Rachel and I not as close as you guys?! I mean do we not have as much fun?! Don’t I deserve a few tears?!! I mean we-we told Joey, he cried his eyes out!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! I did not cry my eyes out!! Come on! It’s like the end of an era! No more J-man and Channie’s!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, I gotta ask, who calls us that?!

scenes.scene_description: Chandler and Joey's, Chandler is packing some books and Joey is watching him.

lines.speaker: Russell
lines.text: You got married again.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Russell
lines.text: So that’s your second marriage in two years.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes, second in two years. Third overall.

lines.speaker: Russell
lines.text: Ross, I have been a divorce attorney for 23 years and never had I so much business from one client. Why don’t you tell me what happened.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Basically, Rachel and I were in Vegas and we got drunk…

lines.speaker: Russell
lines.text: I’m sorry, is this the same Rachel who’s name you said at the altar in the second marriage?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes-yes-yes! But, I-I do not love her.

lines.speaker: Russell
lines.text: Oh, that’s better then.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: This was just a drunken mistake and I need to get it annulled.

lines.speaker: Russell
lines.text: I see. Have you considered therapy?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I think just the annulment for today.

lines.speaker: Russell
lines.text: There are a couple of forms to fill out.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Easy.

lines.speaker: Russell
lines.text: And we’ll need to have witnesses who can testify that you were not of uh, sound mind.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No problem.

lines.speaker: Russell
lines.text: And we’ll need you and Rachel to testify before a judge.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ooh! There’s no way to do this without her? ‘Cause I kinda all ready told her uh, it was, it was already taken care of.

lines.speaker: Russell
lines.text: Of course you did. Look Ross, you can’t get an annulment unless you and Rachel are both there.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh-ha, what about someone who looks like Rachel? I will think about the therapy.

scenes.scene_description: Ross’s lawyer’s office, his name is Russell and has just been told by Ross about his current situation.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey. So um, I was thinking that maybe we should start dividing up our stuff.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Y’know, no point in dragging it out. Dragging out the long process of you moving out and us not living together anymore.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey Rach, what about this? Huh? Who-who gets this? See, I don’t know if I want it because it might be y’know, too many memories!
lines.stage_direction: She holds up a chrome 5-point star.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What the hell is that?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I don’t know. Hey, Rachel, you want the big plate? I want you to have the big plate.
lines.stage_direction: Picks up a big plate from the coffee table.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wow! Mon, thanks! I love this plate!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Something to remember me by!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Mon, honey you’re not dying. I’m just moving out. Y’know, I mean we’re gonna see each other all the time.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: But still, it’s a big change. The end of an era, you might say!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Are you okay? You’re not blinking.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I’m fine! I just, I’m thinking how much it’s an end of era.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, all right. But y’know I gotta say, I don’t, I don’t think six years counts as an era.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: An era is defined as a significant period of time. Now, it was significant to me, maybe it wasn’t significant to you!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What is the matter with you?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What is the matter with you?!! Why aren’t you more upset?! Aren’t you gonna be sad that we’re not gonna be living together anymore?! I mean aren’t you gonna miss me at all?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right, fine, but don’t get mad at me. It’s-it’s just a little hard to believe.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What’s hard to believe?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well y’know, it’s you guys. You-you do this kind of stuff! Y’know? I mean, you-you were gonna get married in Vegas and then you backed out! I guess I’m not upset because I don’t see you guys going through with it. I’m sorry.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Rachel, it’s going to happen. Chandler is gonna move in here.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: But I…

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No-no, wait! Just let me finish, okay? This isn’t something that we just, we just impulsively decided in-in Vegas, this is something we both really want. And it is going to happen.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It is? Really?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, sweetie.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I mean we’re not, we’re not gonna live together anymore?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What? Oh my God! I’m gonna miss you so much!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I’m gonna miss you!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I mean it’s the end of an era!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know!

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's, Rachel is painting her toe nails as Monica enters.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, bye!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh no.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So? Did you get the annulment?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I couldn’t.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I knew it! Because you love Rachel.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It’s not that. Okay? Annulments are more complicated than I…

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, complicated ‘cause of the love.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I… I do, I do not love Rachel. I’m gonna tell her right now about the whole thing so we can get this marriage annulled as fast as possible. Okay? Would I do that if I loved her?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I’ve never been more convinced of your love for her.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I do not have feelings for Rachel! Okay?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ohh, what’s wrong?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Monica and Chandler are really moving in here and I have to move out and everything is changing.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh my—Come here! Come here! It’s okay. Everything’s gonna be okay.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Of course.

scenes.scene_description: The hallway, Ross is coming up the stairs as Phoebe leaves Joey and Chandler’s.
Ross suddenly realizes something.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So, Ross and Rachel got married, Monica and Chandler almost got married, do you think you and I should hook up?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh we do, but not just yet.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Really?! Well, when?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay umm, well, first Chandler and Monica will get married and be filthy rich by the way. Yeah. But it won’t work out.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wow.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I know. Then, I’m gonna marry Chandler for the money and you’ll marry Rachel and have the beautiful kids.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Great!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: But then we ditch those two and that’s when we get married. We’ll have Chandler’s money and Rachel’s kids and getting custody will be easy because of Rachel’s drinking problem.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh-oh, what about Ross?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I don’t want to go into the whole thing, but umm, we have words and I kill him.

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, Joey and Phoebe are talking.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey! Check it out! This is unbelievable! Joey has been holding his breath for almost four minutes!

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's, Joey, Monica, and Phoebe are there as Chandler enters.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Dude! What are yo—you trying to kill me?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Pheebs, I wanna ask you something.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uh-huh, what?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well since I’m movin’ out and-and you’re so beautiful…

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: …how about I move in with you?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, that would be great but then what do we do about Denise?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Who’s Denise?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: My roommate.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You have a roommate?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yes, Denise. Denise!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, what is with the secrecy Phoebe? Huh? And what about this Denise, is she cute?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Pheebs, I don’t understand. How can you have a roommate that none of us know anything about?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Maybe because you never listen to anything that I say. I talk about her all the time! DENISE!!!!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey Rach, listen I was thinkin’ uh, I’m gonna have an extra room over at my place…

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, that’s true.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, why don’t you move in with me? It’ll be great! We could stay up late, watch movies, and you know about Naked Thursday’s right?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, yeah I think I’m gonna find my own place.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey-hey! I thought Naked Thursday’s was just our thing man!

scenes.scene_description: We see Joey who has puffed up his cheeks and Chandler nonchalantly reaches down and pinches Joey’s nose shut. In a few seconds, Joey has to move because he’s now forced to actually hold his breath.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So, what did Rachel say when you told her you were still married to her?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, that. Umm, she took it really well.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You didn’t tell her did you?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Of course not, because you’re in love with her.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I am not in love with her. She was very upset about having to move out so I eh, didn’t tell her we were still married because she would only get more upset. I-I just comforted her, as a friend.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What do you mean, comforted her?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It’s nothing, I just gave her a hug.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ah-ha! A classic sign of love, the hug!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It’s also a sign of friendship.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, not in your case Lovey Loverson.
lines.stage_direction: Tries to take a bite out of Ross’s cookie.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It was a hug!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, just tell me this, did you or did you not smell her hair?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: S-s-smell her hair? What if I did?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ninety percent of a women’s pheromones come out the top of her head! That’s why, that’s why women are shorter. So that men will fall in love when they hug them! Oh come on Ross, you’re a scientist.
lines.stage_direction: Ross is staring at her dumbfounded.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I was hugging her as a friend. It’s not my fault her-her hair got in my face, she’s got a lot of it and it smells all-all uh…coconutty. What?! Oh, that doesn’t mean I have feelings for Rachel! Maybe it means I have feelings for coconuts!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, whatever you say. But just be careful, all right? Rachel’s not in the same place you are.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: If the place you are referring too is being in love, then she is in the same place as me because I am not in that place!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, I didn’t understand that, but y’know, maybe that’s ‘cause you were speaking the secret language of love!

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, Ross is reading a magazine, eating a cookie, and drinking some coffee as Phoebe enters.

She goes for his magazine and he grabs it away before she reaches it. But she was only using the magazine as a decoy because she grabs his cookie and coffee, takes a bite out of the cookie and drinks some of the coffee.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! Dude, some guy just called for you.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Who was it?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don’t know! How about, "Thanks for taking the message." Jeez!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay listen, y’know when you move in Rachel’s room is gonna be empty, you wanna talk about what we want to do with it?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Sure!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, I was thinking we should have a beautiful guest room, right? With a mahogany sleigh bed and bedside tables with flowers on them all the time! And we could have a roll top desk with comment cards on them so people could say how much they loved staying here!! Okay, whatever, I really haven’t thought about it that much.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I like that idea. Obviously! I was thinking maybe-maybe-maybe it could be a game room, y’know? I mean you can buy old arcade games like uh, like Space Invaders and Asteroids for $200, the real ones! The big-big ones!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay so you mean no as in, "Gee Chandler, what an interesting idea. Let’s discuss it before we reject it completely."

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, I’m sorry. Of course I mean that. Interesting idea, umm, talk about it, but no.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So, that’s it?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I just don’t think arcade games go in the beautiful guest room. The beautiful guest room is gonna be filled with antiques.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Which is why Asteroids is perfect! It’s the oldest game!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What do you have against the beautiful guest room?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I don’t have anything against the beautiful guest room, especially since everybody we know lives about 30 seconds away!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Are you mocking me?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, I’m not mocking you, or you beautiful guest room.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's, Chandler and Monica are there as Joey enters.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, what’s up?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Nothing, Monica and I had a stupid fight.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: But you’re still moving in together, right? Because my ad came out today.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: "Wanted. Female roommate, non-smoker, non-ugly." Nice!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah? I just figured y’know, after living with you it’d be an interesting change of pace to have a female roommate, y’know? Someone I can learn from, someone-someone who’s different than me. And what’s more different than me; a guy who’s not 19 than say a girl who is 19? Enh? Not just a hat rack my friend!

scenes.scene_description: Chandler and Joey's, Chandler is coming from across the hall.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Pheebs, I have to ask you…

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Shhhhhh! I’m swamped right now.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You’re just staring into space.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Umm, I’m trying to move that pencil.
lines.stage_direction: There’s a pencil lying on the table.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: This one?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It worked!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Pheebs, this whole apartment thing is just a nightmare! Every place I can afford comes with a roommate who is a freak. I mean, look at this; "Wanted. Female roommate, non-smoker, non-ugly." It’s just, there is nothing! The city’s full!
lines.stage_direction: Points to one and starts to read it.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wait, no, look at this! "Two bedroom, two bath, must be non-smoker, Satan worshipers okay…" Oh, yeah, but it’s on the ground floor.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Rach, uh, you still looking for a place?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah! Why?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, there’s this guy, Warren, from the museum and he’s going on a dig for like two years and he’s got this great place he needs to sublet. So uh, you interested?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That sounds great! I’d love to live at Warren’s!! I love Warren! Thank you!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Don’t thank me! If you wanna thank something, thank the volcano that erupted thousands of years ago, killing but perfectly preserving an entire civilization. Here’s Warren’s number.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, this is great! I am gonna call him right now! Oh, thank you!

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, Phoebe is on the couch staring off into space as Rachel enters.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, you go grab it!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I saw it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I don’t know what you’re talking about.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Umm, I’m talking about that which you already know but won’t admit. You love her again; you re-love her!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look, I do not re-love her.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I can’t believe you won’t just admit it! Okay, just promise me that you won’t do anything stupid.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look, we’re just friends now! Okay? Why would I do anything stupid?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ugh!!! Well, the apartment is already subletted! I mean, this is just hopeless. I’m never gonna find anything.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You can live with me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

scenes.scene_description: She hugs him and he starts to hug her back but notices the look Phoebe is giving him and pushes her away.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God! Are you serious?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh-huh.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I would love to live with you Ross; that’s-that’s great! Thank you!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, I’m-I’m just glad I could, y’know, help you out.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wow! I’m-I’m so happy for you guys. This is so-so, not stupid.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross-Ross, you have no idea what this means to me! I mean, I mean I was gonna be homeless. You just saved me! You’re my hero!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hero, I uh, I don’t know—well, all right.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, I have to go tell Monica what a wonderful brother she has!
lines.stage_direction: Kisses him on the cheek and exits.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh please! You’re gonna say things now, aren’t ya?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No. No, I won’t. But I should tell you this, this exact same thing happened to my roommate Denise. She moved in with a guy who was secretly married to her and he said he didn’t love her, but he really did, and it just blew up! And that’s how she ended up living with me! Okay, that’s a lie.

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, continued from earlier.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hi, listen, I’m sorry about before. I don’t need to have a game room. I mean when I was a kid I only played those games because I couldn’t get girls, and now I can ‘em—Now, I have you. Not-not that I think that I have you or think of you as property in any sort of way, I see women…

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Stop it Chandler. I’m sorry too.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah! Oh yes!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ohh.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Listen, we don’t have to make that a guest room, we can think of something to do with the room together.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That’s a great idea! We can easily think of a way for us both to enjoy the room.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Totally!

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's, Monica is in the kitchen as Chandler enters.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: We don’t have to come up with this now.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh good.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, y’know what? Why don’t we think about changes we can make in the living room?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Changes?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, I mean we’re gonna have to move around some furniture to make room for my chair.
lines.stage_direction: Kisses her and heads into the living room.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You’re-you’re-you’re gonna bring the Barca Lounger over here?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Is that a problem?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, it’s a set and they should probably stay together.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, that’s cool. Then I’ll just bring them both over.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: See now-now you’re taking them away from their home.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, I get it. So, I get nothing! Nothing here is mine! Everything here is yours! I’ll get up in the morning put on your clothes, and head off to work!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah-yeah, y’know what? Yeah, that’s it-that’s it, everything will be mine! Nothing will be yours! That’s-that’s what I said! Oh come on, Chandler! I’m talking about the barca lounger! It just, it doesn’t match! Where is it gonna go?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: In the game room!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Look it is not my fault that your chairs are incredibly ugly!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right! That’s fine! That’s fine! I won’t bring over the chairs! I won’t bring anything over! I wouldn’t want to ruin the ambiance over here at Grandma’s place!!

scenes.scene_description: They start thinking.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Everything on your application looks really good—Ohh! Just one last question umm, are you and your friends gonna be over here all the time like partying and hanging out?
lines.stage_direction: exiting from Chandler’s room with the new roommate

lines.speaker: The Potential Roommate
lines.text: Oh don’t worry, I’m not really a party girl.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Whoa!! Now look, don’t be just blurtin’ stuff out. I want you to really think about your answers. Okay?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You can call off the roommate search! Hi! I’ll be living here.
lines.stage_direction: entering To the potential roommate Heads for the bathroom.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh don’t listen to him, he’s just some guy who really wants the apartment, but I don’t think he’s gonna get it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why did you take the shower curtain down?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That thing was a hazard! I’m very safety conscious.

scenes.scene_description: Chandler and Joey's, Joey is interviewing a potential roommate. And yes, she’s a female, non-smoker and very non-ugly.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey! Oh listen, I was just clearing some space for your stuff.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh thanks, but listen, I was just at Monica’s and she and Chandler had a big fight and they’re not moving in.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What do you mean, they’re not moving in? They-they’re still moving in right?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No-no, they just had a big blowout over what to do with my room.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?! Over a stupid room!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, I feel kinda bad for them, but I’m also really psyched ‘cause I don’t have to move in here!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh no, yeah no, that part’s great!

scenes.scene_description: Ross’s Apartment, Rachel is entering and Ross is making some room on the shelves for his stuff.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What’s all this about you guys fighting?! Is this really over a room?! I mean, that is so silly!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ross, we can handle this.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, apparently not, and I can’t just stand by and watch two people I care about very much be hurt over something that is so silly. I mean, enough of the silliness!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, why don’t you tell her to stop being silly!
lines.stage_direction: Monica mocks him and he joins in.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay-okay! Two very good points, look I’ve known you both a long time, and I’ve never seen either of you one/millionth as happy as you’ve been since you’ve got together. Do you really want to throw that all away over a room? That is so silly. Now wh-what is more important, love or silliness?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, we are fond of the silliness, but we also have a soft spot for the love.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Love is the best medicine.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That’s laughter.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Why do you do it?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I don’t know.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay! All right! Now, Chandler you-you wanna live with Monica, right?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, I do.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And Mon, you wanna live with Chandler, don’t ya?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Good! A verbal contract is binding in the state of New York!

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's, Ross is entering, dragging Chandler, to mediate the argument between Chandler and Monica.
Storms out.

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: So I understand you’re looking for a place.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No-no, I’m staying put.

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: Oh, I was going to offer you my apartment.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Why, where are you going?

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: I don’t know.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey Rach!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You’re never gonna believe it uh, Monica and Chandler are moving in again. That’s great news right—I mean for them. Right?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh wow.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah but, on the bright side, we get to be roommates again.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah. Y’know umm, uh, umm, about that, umm, Ross I really appreciate your offer to let me move in and everything, but don’t you think it’s gonna be weird?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wh-why?! Why-why-why would it, why would it be weird?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, because of us! Because of our history.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No! No! It would be weird if we were still in that place, I mean are you still in that place?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No! Not at all!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Good! Me neither! So it’s not a problem. We’re just two friends who happen to be roommates.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, but Ross, eventually you and I are gonna be dating.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Really?! We are?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah! I’m gonna have a boyfriend, you’re gonna have a girlfriend…

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ohh! That would be great.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: But y’know what, if you think it’s gonna be okay we’ll just work out a system. Y’know, it’ll be like college, I’ll hang a hanger on the door and put a sign, "Come back later, I’m gettin’ lucky."

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, I didn’t think of that.
lines.stage_direction: laughs as well, but for a different reason

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, Rachel is sitting at the counter waiting for Gunther, yep Gunther’s back, to refill her coffee.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So are you sure about this whole moving in thing?! I mean it’s a really big step! And-and what’s the rush?!

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's, Chandler is once again being dragged in by Ross so he that he can try to manipulate the situation so that it’s best for Ross, not necessarily what’s best for Monica and Chandler.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That’s very funny!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: He’s being silly, because he knows that we enjoy the silliness!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, I-I-I’m serious, okay? I mean, think about it. You move in, you start fighting over stupid game rooms, next thing y’know you break up!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ross, you were right before, it was just a stupid fight about a room.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, there are no stupid fights!! This isn’t about the room, this is about what the room…represents! And unfortunately, this room could destroy you!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, I'm not so worried.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, no, me neither.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Fine! It’s your life! I just don’t want to see you guys break up! Which you will do if you move in together, but that’s what you want, there’s nothing I can do. DON’T DO IT !!!!!
lines.stage_direction: Starts to storm out mad about his failed attempt at the manipulation of his best friend and sister, but stops and tries one last time. Monica and Chandler just stare at him. Opens the door and tries one more time. Finally leaves.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You still want to move in together right?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Of course!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ross didn’t scare you?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Scared me out of ever wanting to live with him.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Come here, I want to show you something!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh my God! Someone’s killed Square Man!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: This is where I thought the barca lounger could go! You see you could see the TV and it’s still walking distance to the kitchen.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh that’s so sweet! I want to show you something too!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y’know those big-big uh, road signs that say "Merge?"

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Uh-hmm.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y’know? So I was thinking that we could get one of those signs and hang it over our bed. Because, that’s you and I together! Merge!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God! I love that!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Really?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Uh, no!!

scenes.scene_description: They both start laughing at him.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I found you in my bed! How’d you whined up there? You are a mystery! Little black curly hair! Little black curly hair! Little black, little black, little black, little black, little black curly hair…………

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, Phoebe is singing a new song. Yep, the first new Phoebe song of season six, Ross, Joey, and Rachel are also there.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Now if you want to receive e-mails about my upcoming shows, then please give me money so I can buy a computer.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So umm, where are the other guys?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Umm, well let’s see Monica and Chandler are occupied.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Fighting?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, the other thing. I really think it’s great they work things out.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah. There’s no breaking them up, is there?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey, can I borrow the key to your house so I can run across the street and make a copy?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Sure! Here.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Thank you. Now are you sure? Because once I make a copy, there’s no turning back.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, I’m-I’m sure. Yeah, get out of here before I change my mind.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Umm, listen, Ross do you really think this moving in with Rachel is a good idea?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I’ve been back and forth.

lines.speaker: Joey
