lines.stage_direction: She throws it at the garbage without looking, misses, and knocks over a vase and flowers, which fall to the floor and break. She crawls over, disposes of the diaper, picks up the flowers, and the vase. She throws out both the flowers and the vase. She turns around and only counts two babies. She starts looking for her She turns around and finds that Leslie has managed to crawl into the bottom drawer of the TV cabinet. She picks her up and notices a surprise is waiting in the drawer. Closes the drawer with her foot. To Leslie They both have disappeared as well.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: How do you feel?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, let’s just say that Krog will be fully equipped to destroy the universe again in twelve to fourteen hours.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, so I totally took care of the babies all by myself! I fed ‘em, bathed ‘em, and put ‘em to bed.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And protected them from a tornado?

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's, Phoebe is being triple teamed.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I know, the babies are asleep.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Phoebe, what, what happened here?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I did it! I took care of the babies all by myself!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: But my apartment!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Was the setting of Phoebe’s triumph.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: But the mess!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Is not as important as the fact that Phoebe took care of the babies all by herself.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You’re right, you’re right I shouldn’t freak out. ‘Cause this is what will happen when you and I have babies! When will that be?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Phoebe, would you take a look at this mess!!!!

scenes.scene_description: Monica enters and her jaw drops in horror.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh honey thank God you’re home, I was getting worried.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I picked up the divorce papers. Uh, I’ve already signed everything and I put little ‘X’s where you need to sign.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, little ‘X’s! Great! That makes up for everything!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Y’know, I-I—you’ve done a lot of stupid stuff too! Okay?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, name one stupid thing that is as stupid as this one!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, how about you flew to London to stop my wedding! Ah, how about you told me you loved me after I was already married!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey! Wait a minute! That was different! I did those things because I was in love with you!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah! Right!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You’re right. That’s very different. So let’s, let’s just sign the papers. All right? What?
lines.stage_direction: Sits down and Rachel keeps standing there.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Nothing.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, can we just sign please?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh-hmm.
lines.stage_direction: Just as Rachel finishes signing her name, Ross yanks each page out of the way.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Congratulations.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay Ross, we’re—wait a minute. Umm, I uh, I kinda have a little confession.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, y’know this whole marriage thing, kinda my idea.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Excuse me?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, remember how we were too drunk to remember anything the night we were married?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And uh, yeah, I didn’t really, I didn’t want to say anything, but it kinda it just, it kinda kept coming back to me, and umm, remember we were in the casino and for some reason thought it would be funny to eat a lot of grapes. And uh, and I thought it would be funnier if we got married. So as a, as a compromise we decided first to get married, and then to eat a lot of grapes. So umm, sorry I got us into this mess.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So then if-if—I mean if you think about it, this is all your fault.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, don’t push it though.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I’ve got to say; I know I divorce a lot of women, never thought I would be divorcing you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know. I always thought if you and I got married, it would be the one that stuck. And it wouldn’t be a secret, and we wouldn’t have our wedding dinner at Pizza Hut .

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Did I, did I even treat?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, it was on the house, it was, it was a newlywed special.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That may be the most depressing thing I’ve heard in my life. I should probably get these to my lawyer’s office.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey, thanks Ross, for taking care of all of this.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Eh, no problem.

scenes.scene_description: Ross’s apartment, Rachel is packing what she still has over there as Ross enters.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I’m gonna need a copy of those.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Totally.

scenes.scene_description: They hug.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! Hey! Be careful around my Porsche!

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Hi Joey.
lines.stage_direction: the same one from before approaches

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! How you doin’?

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: He has the most amazing Porsche under there!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I’d love to show ya, but I just tucked her in. She’s sleeping. Hey uh, would you two girls like to go for a drink?
lines.stage_direction: The women both laugh Just then the same guy with the football dives to make a catch, lands on the car cover, and collapses it. It turns out that Joey set up a bunch of boxes to make it look like a Porsche.

scenes.scene_description: The street, Joey is messing with a car cover and still wearing the Porsche stuff. This guy playing street football catches a pass next to the car cover Joey is fooling around with.

lines.speaker: Directed by
lines.text: David Schwimmer

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay! Listen, I’m gonna be moving out so you will be in charge of paying the rent.
lines.stage_direction: watches Joey for a moment Joey quickly moves the hockey stick so that he’s scratching his back with it.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Right! And when is that due?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: First of the month.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: And that’s every month?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, just the months you actually want to live here.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ahhh.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, here is the phone bill.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh my God!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That’s our phone number. Now look, I know I kinda sprung this whole me moving out on thing, so why don’t I just—why don’t I just cover you for a while?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No-no! No way! Joey Tribbiani does not take charity…anymore.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It’s not charity, Joe…

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No! Forget it! Okay—I mean thanks, but I’m done taking money from you. All right, I can take of myself. Now, what’s next? Come on.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay uh, here’s the electric bill.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: This is how much we pay for electric?!!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Uh, yeah.

scenes.scene_description: Chandler and Joey's, Joey is balancing a mini hockey stick on his hand as Chandler enters from his room carrying a bunch of bills.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So, we’ll do the rest of the bills later then?

scenes.scene_description: Joey runs over and shuts off the lights.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So is Joey going to have to give up the apartment?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, I hope not! I tried to offer him some money, but he wouldn’t take it.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, how much do you think he needs?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I figure that $1,500 would cover him for a few months, y’know? But I have to trick him into taking it so I won’t hurt his pride.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Why don’t you hire him as an actor? You could have him dress up and put on little skits. Whatever you want.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well that would help the pride thing.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey! Wow! You look great! Wanna move in with me tomorrow?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay! So, what do you girls have planned for tonight?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, instead of being sad that tonight is my last night together with Rachel we thought we’d go out to dinner and celebrate the fact that Rachel is moving in with Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: And also, my birthday.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It’s not your birthday.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What a mean thing to say! I would never tell you it’s not your birthday!

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's, Chandler is talking to Phoebe about trying to give Joey some money.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey! So you guys have anything planned for the big last night?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, instead of just hanging out, we figure we’d do nothing.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Whoa-whoa-whoa, Knicks season opener tonight. I thought maybe you guys would come over and watch it.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don’t know Ross, not if you’re gonna talk about how you gave up a career in basketball to become a paleontologist.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I did give up a career in basketball to become a paleontologist!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Pheebs, I was wondering…

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You’re not dressed yet?! We’re supposed to start having fun in 15 minutes!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well and clearly not a minute sooner.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Rachel, you are packed though right, I mean please tell me that you’re packed.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Of course I packed! Monica relax! I just wanted to ask Phoebe her opinion on what I should wear tonight.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: My God, I can’t get a minute of peace around this place.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God! Monica’s just gonna kill you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, yeah, I know.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, what you’re wearing is fine for that.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Rachel, I need to borrow— You’re not packed !!!! You’re not packed even a little bit!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Surprise!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, no don’t get mad because look—this is what happened. So I-I started packing, then I realized, "What am I doing? I am lousy at packing!" Right? But you love packing! So, as a gift to you, on our last night, ta-da!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I’ll be coordinator! Oh my God! I’m so sorry, I didn’t get you anything! Okay, look everybody has to help! Okay? You can help, can’t you Phoebe?
lines.stage_direction: grabs a bag of those Styrofoam peanuts

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I have plans.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You’re plans were with us.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That’s right.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, Chandler can make boxes, Ross can wrap, and Joey can lift things. Now Phoebe, go tell the guys they have to help out!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay! Oh my God, thank you!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hurry! Monica’s gonna make you pack! She’s got jobs for everyone! Now, it’s too late for me, but save your selves!
lines.stage_direction: The guys scramble for the door.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay! The movers will be here in 11 hours. Rachel has not packed. Now, everybody has to help! Chandler, we’re gonna start with…
lines.stage_direction: entering and interrupting the guy’s escape attempt

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh nope, I-I have plans with Joey.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I thought you said you were going to do nothing.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, but for the last time.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay fine, now Ross…

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, but-but I can’t do it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I’ve got Ben.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Because, because I’ve got Ben.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It’s almost 8 o’clock, it’s almost past his bedtime. Where-where is he?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: He’s at a dinner party.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Is he really coming? Because I can see right into your apartment!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Of course he is! What, do you think I’d just use my son as-as an excuse? What kind of father do you think I am?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, sorry.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I gotta go make a fake Ben.

scenes.scene_description: Joey and Ross enter.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Here it is! Our last pizzas together as roommates.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, I wish I’d know you were going to do that, I ordered Chinese.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh that’s okay. Hey, actually in a way it’s kinda nice. Me, bringing the food of my ancestors, you, the food of yours!

scenes.scene_description: Chandler and Joey's, Joey is entering carrying two pizzas.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Say, Joe, I had a strange idea of what we could do for our last night. What do you say we play a little uh, foosball for money?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What, are you crazy? You haven’t beaten me once since my injury plagued ’97 season. It would be easier if you just give me your money.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes it would. What do you say to $50?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, you’re on.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, let’s play! The big game, Italy vs. China, apparently.

scenes.scene_description: Chandler stares at him, dumbfounded, then finally agrees.
They start playing.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohhhhhh, look it’s the roller blades.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh God!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You remember when we got these?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I guess you weren’t there.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You guys, we said we were gonna have fun! Come on, hey, remember the time… You don’t remember?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I’m sorry Pheebs, I guess I’m just really said that I’m leaving.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I’m gonna miss you so much.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, this doesn’t have to be so sad though. Y’know? Maybe instead of just thinking about how much you’re gonna miss each other, you should like think of the things you’re not gonna miss.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I don’t think there’s anything.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Come on, there’s gotta be something.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Nope, she’s perfect.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I have one.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Good! Great! You can go first.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh well, I guess I’m not gonna miss the fact that you’re never allowed to move the phone pen.
lines.stage_direction: Laughs. Monica lags behind the laugh a little bit.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Good that’s a good one. Okay, Monica, anything? Y’know? Does Rachel move the phone pen?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Aw, sometimes. Always, actually.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, good. There you go. Doesn’t anyone feel better?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Not just the phone pen. I never get my messages.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You get your messages!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, well I don’t think it really counts if you have to read them off the back of your hand after you fall asleep on the couch.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So-so, you missed a message from who? Chandler or your mom? Or Chandler? Or your mom?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Great! It worked! No one’s sad.

scenes.scene_description: Rachel’s bedroom, she’s in her closet bringing down her pair of roller blades from a top shelf.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yes! I win again! Ha-ha! That’s like 500 bucks you owe me! Whoo-ho-hoo! $500 that is a loooot of electricity! Whoo-ho-ho! I gotta buy some food.
lines.stage_direction: scores Goes over to the fridge and starts opening and closing the door rapidly. By the way, there’s nothing in the fridge. Notices the sparseness of the fridge.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, give me a chance to win my money back. Okay? Sudden death, one goal, $1,000.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You serious?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh yes!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, get ready to owe me!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, here we go. Ready?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No! No! No! No——one can beat me.

scenes.scene_description: Chandler and Joey's, they are just finishing up another game of foosball.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: See? Now, that’s why only the little fake men are supposed to do all the kicking.

scenes.scene_description: In frustration, Joey kicks the table, breaking it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hello? Oh, hi Ross! See? Other people call me!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ooh, your brother. Score!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What’s up?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, I’m just over here with Ben. I thought we’d say hi.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, put him on!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ben, say hi to Aunt Monica. Oh, I guess he doesn’t feel like talking right now. He’s smiling though! Okay, talk to you later.
lines.stage_direction: He holds the phone to the fake Ben he has created out of a pumpkin.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's, the phone rings and Monica answers it as Rachel and Phoebe move a box into the living room.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, I think it was better when you guys were sad. Hey, uh, remember the roller blades?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You know what else I’m not gonna miss? "I’m Monica. I wash the toilet 17 times a day. Even if people are on it!"

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: "Hi I’m Rachel, is my sweater too tight? No? Oh, I’d better wash it and shrink it!"

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: "I’m Monica, I don’t get phone messages from interesting people. Ever!"

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey! I call her!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: "Oh my God, I love Ross! I hate Ross! I love Ross! I hate Ross!"

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: "Oh my God, I can’t find a boyfriend! So I guess I’ll just stumble across the hall and sleep with the first guy I find in there!"

scenes.scene_description: Rachel’s bedroom, Rachel is entering carrying two glasses of wine. She gives one to Phoebe, keeps one, and completely ignores Monica.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, we should get a move on if we wanna make those dinner reservations.
lines.stage_direction: Phoebe dumps a drawer full of makeup into a box.

scenes.scene_description: Monica picks up one of those boxes of hair curling things, dumps it in a box, and storms out.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Still broken?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: This sucks man! The last night you’re here and I lose the two most important things in my life, the foosball table and $500.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, there are other ways of winning back your money, how about a little uh, a little Blackjack?
lines.stage_direction: Holds up a deck of cards.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Nah, not my game.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, uh, how about, how about—y’know what? We could play a new game. A new game, it’s fun.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, what’s it called?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Cups.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don’t know how to play Cups.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I’ll teach ya! Come on, come on, it’s really easy and really, really fun.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, here you go. I have two queens, what do you have?
lines.stage_direction: He deals out two cards each.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: A two and a five.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ho-ho, you win! 50 dollars!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Really?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh yeah! Okay, let’s play again. What do you got?
lines.stage_direction: He deals out two cards each again.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: A four and a nine.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You’re kidding right?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No. Why?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well that’s a full cup!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Damn! I am good at Cups!

scenes.scene_description: Chandler and Joey's, Joey is examining the broken foosball table as Chandler enters from his room.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I’m not talking to her!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, one of you has to take the first step! And it should be you, because she’s the one who’s leaving. It’s harder for her!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, maybe you’re right—She made fun of my phone pen!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I know, I took it hard too.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's, Monica is whining to Phoebe about Rachel.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Listen Rachel, I feel really bad about—What are you doing?
lines.stage_direction: She sees that Rachel is unpacking.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I’m unpacking!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I’m not moving! Is that picture straight?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It needs to go about 20 blocks to the left!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey, y’know what? You’re the one who wants to make this big change and move in with Chandler! You should be the one to go! Why should I have to leave?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Because it’s my apartment!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well it’s mine too! What else you got?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: How about, you’re moving!!
lines.stage_direction: Grabs a bunch of clothes and throws them into a box. What follows is a brief sequence of Rachel unpacking and Monica packing the same stuff over and over again as Rachel chants "No." and Monica chants "Yes."

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Look! This is ridiculous. We should be packing you!!
lines.stage_direction: She knocks over Monica, grabs a box, and runs into the kitchen.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey, how are you guys doing?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Great! Monica’s moving!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I am not!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh really?! Then how come all your stuff is in this box?!
lines.stage_direction: Monica starts chasing Rachel around the table.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, you guys. You guys I think I know what’s going on here. Okay, you guys… STOP!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No Phoebe I am mad!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, deep-deep-deep down!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, I’m just mad!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Then keep running.

scenes.scene_description: Monica goes into Rachel’s room.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You win.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, what did you have?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It doesn’t matter because nothing beats a three and a six. That my friend is D-Cup. Okay, now much have you won so far?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh, wow, 700.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Not 700 exactly?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Double it!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well you see in Cups, once you get $700, you have to double it.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, I didn’t make up the rules. Now, after you receive the doubling bonus, you get uh, one card. Now that one card could be worth $100 bringing your total to 1,500. Don’t get to excited because that’s not gonna happen unless you get—No way!
lines.stage_direction: Joey gets excited. He takes the top card, which is the two of clubs. Of course, any card would’ve won. Chandler pays him.

scenes.scene_description: Chandler and Joey's, they’re still playing Cups.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, this is the last box of your clothes. I’m just gonna label it, "What were you thinking?"

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Funny, because I was just gonna go across the hall and write that on Chandler.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey you guys, I don’t mean to make things worse, but umm, I don’t want to live with Rachel anymore.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You’re just so mean to each other! And I don’t want to end up like that with Rachel. I still like you!

lines.text: So why don’t you just let me worry about making the trifle and you just worry about eating it, alright?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh I am!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, fine!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ross! Can I talk to you for a second?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, uh, can it wait a second Joey? I have to tell my parents something. No it can’t? Okay.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Chandler’s, Time lapse. Jack is still on the couch, picking his teeth with his feet on the table. Phoebe and Judy are sitting on the table looking at him. Ross and Chandler are sitting by the window talking. Rachel and Joey are in the kitchen fooling with Rachel’s trifle. Monica walks into the living room from the kitchen.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, look, I think we have to tell Rachel she messed up her dessert.

lines.text: Look, when everyone eats that...that...Banana-Meat thing, they’re all gonna’ make fun of her, do you want that?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, okay, we’ll just get everyone to act like they like it. That-that way noone makes fun of her and we still get to go to Sweet Potatoe Pie!

lines.text: Dude, they’re not objects.

lines.text: Okay...

lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And um...You look nice today.

lines.text: Oh no. No Ross, don’t do this.

lines.text: What?

lines.text: I just- I don’t think us getting back together is a good idea.

lines.text: Eh?

lines.text: I thought this might happen today. Ross, I know the holidays can be rough. Y’know? And it’s probably really hard for you to be alone right now.

lines.text: You’re alone.

lines.text: No, I-I live with Phoebe. I mean you’re [pity-tone] alone , alone. And I just-it’s just not the time for us. I’m sorry.

lines.stage_direction: just trying to get out of the conversation

lines.text: Oh and Okay, and uh if anyone needs help pretending to like it, I learned something in acting class, try uh, rubbing your stomach or uh, or saying “mmm” and uh, oh oh! And smiling , okay?

lines.text: Yeah, I’m not gonna pay for those acting classes anymore.

lines.text: Really?

lines.text: No, I’m just kidding I would never do that to you! Okay, everybody, it’s trifle time!

lines.text: It sure is.

lines.text: I can’t have any. You know I don’t eat meat. Ohhh no.

lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh oh oh, wait! You only got whipped cream in there! Ya gotta take a bite with all the layers!

lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Op! Wait, you dropped a pea.

lines.text: It’s so good, that I feel really selfish about being the only one who’s eating it, that I think we should have everyone taste how good it is. Especially Ross.

lines.text: Mmm.

lines.text: Okay, now what was that all about? Is it-does it not taste good? Let me try it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wha? No no! Ah! All gone! So good! Maybe Chandler has some left.

lines.text: It tastes like feet!

lines.text: I like it.

lines.text: Are you kidding?

lines.text: ...So a bird just grabbed it, and then tried to fly away with it and, and then just dropped it on the street?

lines.text: Rachel, come here. Okay, I was just starting to take my Thanksgiving nap, and I had another dream about Jack.

lines.text: Oh, Phoebe, do I wanna hear this?

lines.text: I dunno, let’s see! So, okay, I dreamt that we were gonna get married, and he left, becuz he had to go fight a fire. And, um, so okay, I went to a night club, and I saw him making out with a girl.

lines.text: Oh my God, he dream-cheated on you!

lines.text: Yeah, but then Jacques Cousteau came and he kicked his ass for betraying me! It was soo cool! Then, he took me diving and he introduced me to his pet seahorse, who, by the way, was totally coming on to me, and please, that is not gonna happen.

lines.text: Boy, I’m glad I wore the big belt today.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Five minutes ago, a line like that would’ve floored me. Now nothing. Well, not nothing, I am still a woman.

lines.text: Ross? Let’s go.

lines.text: And Dad, y’know that mailman that you got fired? He didn’t steal your Playboys! Ross did!

lines.text: Ross hasn’t worked at the museum for a year!

lines.text: Ross married Rachel in Vegas! And got divorced! Again!!!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I love Jacques Cousteau!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I wasn’t supposed to put beef in the trifle!
lines.stage_direction: reading the recipe magazine, finally figuring out that...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I wanna gooooooo!

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: That’s alot of information to get in in thirty seconds! Alright Joey, if wanna leave, just leave. Rachel, no you weren’t supposed to put beef in the trifle. It did not taste good. Phoebe, I’m sorry, but I think Jacques Cousteau is dead. Monica, why you felt you had to hide the fact that you were in an important relationship is beyond me.
lines.stage_direction: rubbing her temples Phoebe makes a sad face.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: And we kinda figured about the porch swing.

lines.text: Ross, drugs? Divorced? Again?

lines.text: What happened son?

The director shakes his head.

lines.text: Hey! Tall guy! Hey, listen, I wanted to talk to you about that girl that you’re dancing with.

lines.speaker: Tall Guy
lines.text: She’s nice, huh? To think I almost brought my wife to this!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, yeah, okay. Uh, look buddy, I came with that girl, and I had this plan to kiss her at the new years countdown – I’m trying to win her over, so I was wondering if..

lines.speaker: Tall Guy
lines.text: No I don’t think so.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh come on man, you can dance with my partner, she’s real, uh, mellow!

lines.speaker: Tall Guy
lines.text: Look, are you dating this girl you came with?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, I was hoping after tonight that maybe I could you know…

lines.speaker: Tall Guy
lines.text: No, no. She’s fair game if you ask me, sorry buddy!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Alright, alright, hey y’know fair is fair, if you’re right, you’re right, what can I say, but hey oh no!
lines.stage_direction: he pretends to wash his hands He throws water on the guy’s pants

lines.speaker: Tall Guy
lines.text: God! What are you, in second grade?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, now you’re the one who wet his pants.
lines.stage_direction: He throws another handful on him and runs out

scenes.scene_description: The Men’s Bathroom, the tall guy is there as Joey enters.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Man, this sucks! Y’know if Mom and Dad don’t see us on TV after we made them so jealous, I mean, who’s gonna be the losers then?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, I know what’ll get us up on a platform!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: The routine!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ross, we haven’t done the routine since middle school.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, when the snippy guy sees the routine, he’ll wanna build us our own platform!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Was it really that good?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: We got honourable mention in the brother/sister dance category! Look, it’s almost fake midnight, do we really have any other choice?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, let’s do it. Mom and Dad are gonna be so faced!

scenes.scene_description: The Set, Monica and Ross are dancing.
They move into a space, and Ross points to his eyes and then to hers
They start the routine. However hard I try, I really cannot describe it. You will have to watch it. I’m sorry.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So, do we really have to ask who’s going up on the platform next?

lines.speaker: Director
lines.text: Oh no! You get up there and do that again exactly like that!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes!
lines.stage_direction: They run over to a platform

lines.speaker: Director
lines.text: Make sure you get this, they’re gonna want it for the bloopers show. Alright cut! Listen up everyone, when we start again it’s gonna be the countdown to new years, so I wanna see everybody’s excitement.

lines.speaker: Tall Guy
lines.text: Hey, pal, you have about three seconds to get away from my partner.

lines.speaker: Director
lines.text: What’s going on over here?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh, take a look at the guy’s pants! I mean, I know you told us to show excitement, but don’t you think he went a little overboard?

lines.speaker: Director
lines.text: What’s the matter with you? Get out of here!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, take a hike wetpants!

scenes.scene_description: When they finish, they walk over to the director

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Can you believe this? We’re gonna be on the platform for the Millennium moment!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know! Hey, you haven’t been practising the routine, have you?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Me too!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, when the music starts up again, I was thinking of maybe goind into the robot, y’know?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ross, I think we should stick to the routine, we don’t wanna look stupid!

lines.speaker: Director
lines.text: Alright we’re back! Ten seconds left here we go! Ten! Nine! Eight! Seven!
lines.stage_direction: He continues to count as we hear Joey think

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Three, Two, One!

lines.speaker: Director
lines.text: Cut!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No! Year! Happy No Year!

lines.speaker: Director
lines.text: Okay, here’s where we go to the live shot of Times Square, nice work everyone that’s a wrap!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey! Look who I found!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, hey guys!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Y’know, birds have a very good sense of direction, and I thought maybe they could help us find where the presents are hidden.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, if the presents are hidden south for the winter.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Or we could just follow your clever jokes – any ideas? No! Didn’t think so! Okay, c’mon guys, show us where the presents are!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, the duck seems to think that Monica got me garbage! Hmm, I wonder what I could get Monica that’s as good as garbage?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hmm, how about my azzz?

lines.text: This bench, it’s hollow! I can’t believe I never knew that! Oh, the presents!!!

lines.text: What?

lines.text: Alright, no, we could look at them!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, this one’s for me!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, this one’s for Chandler. Here.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh great!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: And the big one’s for me!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ooh, let’s open them!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay! Okay! Okay!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hee hee!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Wait, we can’t do this.

lines.speaker: Phoebe and Rachel
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I don’t wanna know what Monica got me. Y’know? I mean, look, I’m sure she worked really hard at getting you a present, and wanting to surprise me, and you guys are gonna ruin that, and I, look we have to put these back, this is not what Christmas is about.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Whatever Linus, I’m opening mine.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Nobody is opening anything ok? Look, I don’t know about you guys, but I wanna see the look on Monica’s face when I give her my present, and I’m sure she wants to see the look on my face when I get mine. So please, please, can we just, can we put them back?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Will you get us better gifts?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Fine!

scenes.scene_description: Rachel sits on the window seat, and knocks against it

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey guys! You found the presents? Chandler, you let them find the presents? Great! Do you know how long it took me to find you that water purifier?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That’s what you got me?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh yes, I see what you mean. That look is priceless.

scenes.scene_description: Monica enters, and they throw the gifts behind them.
Rachel leans over to look at him.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Home sweet home, huh? Nice to, uh, get back to reality. Plus we know how the New Year’s gonna go off. I guess there’s no reason for all that Y2K panic, y’know? Anyway, g’night!

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: Joey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: 3, 2, 1. Happy New Year.
lines.stage_direction: She takes his hands She kisses him

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, yoii. What was that for?

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: Well, I don’t know tonight when they yelled cut and we didn’t get to kiss, I was really, really, disappointed, and I just, really wanted to kiss you

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Really? In the moment, I really wanted to kiss you too. In the moment.

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: In the moment, yeah.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: But only in the moment. So do ya wanna kiss again?

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: Sure, New Year’s Eve is only two weeks away. Can you wait?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: Me neither.

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: Joey, you don’t have to count down every time we kiss.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh, yeah, okay. Except I sorta felt like I needed a couple of seconds to get ready.

scenes.scene_description: Joey and Janine’s apartment, they both enter.
They kiss to the music of Auld Lang Syne

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We were on the platform, ready to dance the world into the new Millennium, and the guy yelled ‘CUT!’

lines.text: Uh, wait, so you guys are telling me you actually did the routine from eighth grade?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah. But of course we had to update it a little bit. Hey, by the way, great thinking about catching me!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: ‘Cos I was gonna say there’s no way you could’ve done the end the way you guys did it back then!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What? We could do it!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I don’t know, I mean you were a lot bigger, I mean, stronger back then.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I can do it, okay? Come on, let’s go.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I can’t do it!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Now you do that, you’re on TV.

lines.text: Phoebe hates Pottery Barn ?!

lines.text: Tomorrow!

lines.text: Blah?!

lines.text: Uh, they’re like my best friends. Are you saying we can’t hang out with them? ‘Cause that would kinda be a problem.

lines.text: No! Of course we can still hang out with them. Just y’know, not two nights in a row. Okay?

lines.text: I guess.

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: If you want, I’ll sell my friends and use the money to buy you presents.

lines.text: I know! I know, she says it’s all mass-produced, nothing is authentic, and everyone winds up having the same stuff. So come on, she’s gonna be here any second! Can we please just cover this up with something?! Please?

lines.text: What? No! No! I am not gonna hide it from Phoebe—Ooh, although I did get some great Pottery Barn sheets!

lines.text: Hey!

lines.text: Hey!

lines.text: I don’t think so.

lines.speaker: The Fan
lines.text: No-no, it was! She was in Sex Toy Story 2, Lawrence of Alabia, and I got her autograph! The guys at the comic book store aren’t gonna believe this!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey Gunther, don’t let that guy in here anymore! He just said Phoebe’s a porn star!

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: Well, I wouldn’t call her a star, but she’s really good. You should check out Inspecther Gadget.

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, Phoebe is putting away her guitar as a man approaches.

lines.speaker: Jill
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Jill
lines.text: You’ll never believe what just happened, Ross just totally blew me off and he didn’t even tell me why!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohhh well. Y’know what honey? The best thing to do to get over a guy is to start dating someone else. Oh! There is this great guy you will love at work named Bob! He’s a real up-and-comer in Human Resources.

lines.speaker: Jill
lines.text: Y’know, thanks for trying to cheer me up, but I’m not gonna date some random guy from your work.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It’s not random, it’s Bob.

lines.speaker: Jill
lines.text: It’s probably because not mature enough. Or smart enough. Maybe he doesn’t like the way I dress—No that can’t be it. It’s really gotta be the smart thing. Oh I’m so stupid! I’m just like this incredibly pretty stupid girl!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No honey, okay, okay, you wanna know why Ross canceled the date? Because I asked him to.

lines.speaker: Jill
lines.text: You asked him too?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hm-mmm.

lines.speaker: Jill
lines.text: Why?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Because you are my sister and Ross and I have this huge history…

lines.speaker: Jill
lines.text: I don’t understand, do you want to go out with Ross?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Jill
lines.text: You don’t want him, but you don’t want me to have him?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Y’know Bob in Human Resources…

lines.speaker: Jill
lines.text: Ugh! I cannot believe you did this too me! You had me doubting how smart I was! You had me doubting my fashion sense!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Look, this is not that big of a deal! You just don’t date Ross! There’s a million other guys out there, you just…

lines.speaker: Jill
lines.text: Hey! You have no right to tell me what to do.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I’m not telling you what to do! I am telling you what not to do!

lines.speaker: Jill
lines.text: Why are you so jealous of me?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Jill this is not about me being jealous of you! This is about you being a brat! Wanting what you can’t have!

lines.speaker: Jill
lines.text: Can’t have?! Excuse me, the only thing I can’t have is dairy !

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, time lapse, Rachel is at the counter as Jill enters.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right, all right, well you just blew your chances at dating Bob!

lines.speaker: Jill
lines.text: Who?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: In Human Resources !!!!!!

scenes.scene_description: Starts to storm out.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: …I am jealous of her?! I mean who does she think she is?! Princess Caroline?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You’re jealous of Princess Caroline?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Do I have my own castle?

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Chandler's, Rachel is ranting about Jill to Chandler and Monica.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey! Uh, Phoebe’s not here is she?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh great! Did you get a movie?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uhhh, yeah. Yeah. But uh, I don’t think it’s the kind you’re gonna like.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You didn’t get more movies that are gonna have us reaching for the tissues all night did you?

scenes.scene_description: Joey and Ross enter.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Sort of…

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Guys, what’s going on?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Phoebe’s a porn star!

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: What?!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe Buffay in Buffay
lines.text: The Vampire Layer.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That’s Phoebe! Where did you get that?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well down at the adult video place down on Bleaker.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And-and I, and I saw that Joey was about to go in, so I ran in ahead of him to-to surprise him and, and then I pretended I didn’t know he was in there.
lines.stage_direction: They all kinda look at him.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wow! I mean, I just—I can’t, I can’t believe this. Y’know, I mean you think you know someone even, even Phoebe who’s always been somewhat of a question mark.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: This is so bizarre. I guess it kinda makes sense though, y’know she had such a terrible childhood.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, I had a terrible childhood and I don’t do porn.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes, but you are dead inside.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right well, I’d better take that back.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wh-what, why?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: We can’t watch that! I mean that’s Phoebe!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah you’re right, we can’t—we shouldn’t watch this.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Absolutely not.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Probably just the first half.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No! Hey no! This is wrong you guys! Phoebe’s our friend! Well, I’m not gonna watch it!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah! Good for you Joe!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah.

scenes.scene_description: They all run over to Joey and Ross, Chandler grabs the movie and reads the title.

lines.speaker: Buffay, the Vampire Layer
lines.text: Ah, I thought I’d find you here, Nasforatool.

lines.speaker: The Vampire
lines.text: Buffay, are you going to plunge your stake into my dark places?

lines.speaker: Buffay, the Vampire Layer
lines.text: Actually, I was kinda hoping it would be the other way around.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hold on a second! What is that on her ankle?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Her ankle is what you’re watching?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well it’s hard to tell… Oh God, if she would just stop moving.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: She’s just doing her job!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You sick bastards!
lines.stage_direction: sitting at the kitchen table with his back to the TV

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, it’s a tattoo! That’s weird, Phoebe doesn’t… Wait that’s Ursula! That’s not Phoebe that is Ursula!

scenes.scene_description: At this point, Buffay, the Vampire Layer and Nasforatool start to get it on. Of course, since this is network TV, we can only see the reactions of the gang to the film playing off screen.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Re! Re! Then I can watch that! Rewind it! Rewind it!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey! What’s up? Oh my God! What am I doing?!!

scenes.scene_description: Upon hearing this, Joey can’t turn his chair around fast enough and knocks it over.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey sweetie!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey!
lines.stage_direction: He quickly tries to hide the book by throwing it under the couch, only the couch has no back and it slides into the kitchen.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: There’s no back to this couch!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Why are you reading this? You hate this kind of stuff.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah I know, but I figured a shot y’know? Maybe one of those stories would make me cry and then you wouldn’t think I was y’know, all dead inside.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh that’s so sweet! Look Chandler I don’t care if you can’t cry, I love you.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh that makes me feel so warm in my hollow tin chest.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Stop it!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, I mean, come on, seriously think about it, we get married, we’re up at the altar and I’m like this.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I won’t care, because I know you will be feeling it all in here.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Chandler's, Chandler is reading Chicken Soup for the Soul as Monica enters.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah! And if, and if we have a baby one-day, and the doctor hands it to you in the delivery room and you don’t cry, so what! And-and-and, and if we take him to college and come home and see his empty room for the first time, and you got nothing, it won’t matter to me.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, well I won’t uh, worry about this anymore then.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: And-and-and if I die, from a long illness. And you’re writing out my eulogy and you open a desk drawer and you find a note from me that says, "I will always be with you," and you still can’t shed one tiny tear, I know you’ll be crying a river inside.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Aww, I love you so…

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What is wrong with you?!!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?! You can’t shed a tear for your dead wife!! Now, I left you a note from the beyond!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So you didn’t mean any of that?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No you robot!!

scenes.scene_description: Points to her heart.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You damn kids! You ring my bell one more time, I swear to… Ohh, uh Jill. Umm, that-that’s just a little game I play with the kids down the hall. Umm, they’ve really taken a liking to me. Uhh what’s-what’s-what’s the matter?
lines.stage_direction: Opens the door to find Jill standing there. Quickly looks out to see if they’re watching.

lines.speaker: Jill
lines.text: Rachel and I had a really big fight, can I come in? I-I mean I know we’re not supposed to see each other anymore and I’m okay with that, it’s just that I don’t know anybody in the city and I really need somebody to talk to about it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Of course, what happened?

lines.speaker: Jill
lines.text: I don’t want to talk about it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, umm…

lines.speaker: Jill
lines.text: But you know what might really cheer me up?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Jill
lines.text: Seeing some more of your super-cool slides.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wow! Really?!

lines.speaker: Jill
lines.text: Totally, I love them! And, maybe you could finish telling me about all the different kinds of sand.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, I’d love to! Here, you wait right here and I’ll go get the projector and my notes!

lines.speaker: Jill
lines.text: Oh great! Thanks Ross, you’re such a good friend!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ohh!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey! Have you guys seen Jill? I can’t find her anywhere.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, I haven’t.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, is Ross home? Maybe I’ll just call him to see if he’s actually seen her.

scenes.scene_description: Ross's apartment, his doorbell is ringing and he’s running to answer it while doing up his pants.
She goes to look out the window at Ross’s apartment and sees Jill staring at her and closing the drapes with an evil look on her face. Rachel is stunned into silence.

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Who is it?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It’s Phoebe! Phoebe!

lines.text: Hey!

lines.text: Oh God. So-so you’re making porn movies.

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: No I’m not.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You’re making one right now!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: And- and you’re using my name!

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Yeah, can I help you with something?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Look, I’m talking right now! You’re—you mean her.

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Y’know, twin stuff is always a real big seller.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Yeah, I can talk them into giving you like, 30 dollars.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No!! No way! No! And stop using my name! And shame on you! And shame on all of you! You’re disgusting! Especially you with that!
lines.stage_direction: Yells into the apartment points to someone Storms away.

lines.text: Slides are almost ready.

lines.speaker: Jill
lines.text: Yeah. Ooh, I know what this is missing! Alcohol!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh okay, well there’s-there’s wine in the kitchen.

lines.speaker: Jill
lines.text: Oh great!
lines.stage_direction: Goes to get it as the phone rings.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hello?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: O-okay, Rach calm-calm down, okay? She-she’s really upset we’re just talking.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross! I think she is trying to make something happen with you to get back at me!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So that’s the only reason she could be here huh? It couldn’t have anything to do with the fact that-that maybe I’m a good listener and I uh I put on a great slide show!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross, I am telling you that she is using you to get back at me!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Y’know what? I think I can take care of myself, I’ll talk to you later. Good-bye. Whoa! Uh, that was your sister actually. She-she thinks that you’re just using me.
lines.stage_direction: Hangs up the phone and turns to find Jill sitting really close to him.

lines.speaker: Jill
lines.text: So?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So, I just came from the company Ursula works for.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh no, not you too!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No! No! I just went to pick up Phoebe Buffay’s checks; there were a lot of them.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Nice!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Um-mmm, and I won’t have to go there anymore because I gave them my correct address.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That’s great, but isn’t it gonna bother that people still think you’re a porn star?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh no! No! I know how to handle it.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You do?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah. You’re trying to figure out where you know me from? All right, I’ll give you a hint. From porn! Okay? Yeah your pervert boyfriend watched me in a porno movie! See?
lines.stage_direction: She notices some guy putting a coat on his girlfriend is trying to remember where he’s seen her before. To him He tries to rush his girlfriend out. To Joey

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You uh, you may have been right about Jill.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh! I knew it! What happened?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Umm, she kissed me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?! You kissed!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Maybe we should give them some privacy.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Shhhh!!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look, I uh, I tried not to kiss her, okay?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, it doesn’t sound like it! I mean, it’s pretty easy not to kiss someone, you just don’t kiss them! See look at us, right now, not kissing!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Let me finish, okay? She started kissing me and-and I didn’t stop it. I guess I-I just wasn’t thinking…

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah that’s right you weren’t thinking! Y’know what? Let me give you something to think about!
lines.stage_direction: She pulls up her sleeves and steps towards him.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh wait—hold it! But then I started thinking and I stopped the kissing.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, well thank you for taking your tongue out of my sister’s mouth long enough to tell me that.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look I-I realize if anything were to happen with me and Jill then nothing could ever happen with us!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, I mean, look I don’t know if anything is going to happen with us, again. Ever. But I don’t want to know that it-it never could. So I stopped it and she got mad and broke my projector.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wow. I, I don’t even know what to say. Thank you.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You’re welcome.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Chandler's, time lapse, Ross is entering. Chandler and Monica are at the kitchen table. Rachel is on the couch reading.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God! Are-are you crying?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I just don’t see why those two can’t work things out!

scenes.scene_description: Chandler starts crying.

lines.speaker: Jill
lines.text: All right, I’m leaving! Because I’m not going to spend one more day with someone whose out to sabotage my every move. That’s you Rachel!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, I got that.

lines.speaker: Jill
lines.text: And you! I throw myself at you and you say no, how gay are you?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You take care Jill.

lines.speaker: Jill
lines.text: Okay, see ya!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Bye-bye-e!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Bye.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I-I can’t believe Jill’s gone. I can’t help it, I opened a gate.
lines.stage_direction: starts crying They all look at him.

title: The One That Could Have Been

lines.text: Greg Malins & Adam Chase

lines.text: David Crane & Marta Kauffman

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey, you guys! Guess what? Barry and Mindy are getting a divorce!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wow!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What is the matter with you?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No! Barry and Mindy.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh sorry, I hear divorce I immediately go to Ross. Who-who’s Barry and Mindy?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Barry was the guy that I was almost married and Mindy was my best friend.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ohh-oh, wasn’t he cheating on you with her?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, but that just means that he was falling asleep on top of her instead of me.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Why did they get divorced?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, apparently she caught him cheating on her with someone else. Isn’t that sad? God, could you imagine if I actually married him?! I mean how different would my life be?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I know what you mean, I’ve always wondered how different my life would be if-if I’d never gotten divorced.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Which time?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: The first time! No seriously, imagine if Carol hadn’t realized she was a lesbian.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I can’t. I keep seeing it the good way.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I’d bet I’d still be doing my kara-tay. Towards the end of our marriage I was doing a lot of kara-tay as a way of releasing the tension from y’know, not doing anything else physical.
lines.stage_direction: That’s karate, he’s just saying it that way.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Maybe the problem was you were pronouncing it kara-tay.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: And what if I was still fat? Well, you wouldn’t be dating me, that’s for sure.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Sure I would!

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Oh yeah! Come on! Yeah right!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What, you guys really think that I’m that shallow?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, I just think Monica was that fat.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, imagine if I never got fired off Days Of Our Lives ! Oh-hey, there’s Carol again!
lines.stage_direction: Closes his eyes to do so.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What if I had had the guts to quit my job? I’d probably be writing for the New Yorker , getting paid to be funny. But my job’s fun too! I mean tomorrow, I-I don’t have to wear a tie.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What if I had taken that job at Merrill Lynch ?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Merrill Lynch ?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, I had a massage client who worked there and-and he said I had a knack for stocks.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well why didn’t you take the job?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Because at that time you see, I thought everything that rhymed was true. So I thought y’know that if I’d work with stocks, I’d have to live in a box, and only eat lox, and have a pet fox.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, do you guys think that if all those things happened, we’d still hang out?

lines.text: Oh, I’m sorry. Ross Tillman.

lines.text: Ohh, of course Monica’s brother!

lines.text: Good-good, I’m-I’m married.

lines.text: Ohh! Me too!

lines.text: Ohh, I would love too.

lines.speaker: Ross

lines.text: Ohh! Okay!

lines.text: Oh yeah? Okay.

lines.text: Okay.

lines.text: Oh I just got another rejection letter. They said my writing was funny, just not " Archie Comic funny."

lines.text: Y’know what honey? I got to get back to the hospital.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Monica's Boyfriend
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Bye.

lines.speaker: Monica's Boyfriend

lines.text: Hey man, look sorry about that Archie thing. Do uh, do you need me to give you some money?

lines.text: Hey, I may have no money, but I still have my pride.

lines.text: Ehh.

lines.text: That’s an idea! Hey, if I hired an assistant, would-would you take money from her?

lines.text: No Joey! Chandler could be your assistant! See, he could answer all of your fan mail and stuff!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That’s great! That would be great! Let’s do that!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I could use the money; it could give me time to write.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh right great! Welcome aboard!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right! Now hey, I need to use the bathroom. Since I don’t need any assistance in there, take a break!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right!

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, Joey, Fat Monica, and her boyfriend are sitting on the couch. Monica’s boyfriend is getting up to get something. For future reference, for the rest of this episode Monica’s fat, I won’t be calling her Fat Monica throughout.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey Phoebe! Guess what?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ohh that’s so sweet! Oh! Hang on! Hang onnnnnn!!! Go!! No! No-no! I said sell when it hits 50! 5-0, it’s a number! It comes after 4-9!! No, it’s okay. It’s okay, you’re allowed one mistake. Just kidding, you are of course fired.

scenes.scene_description: As Joey goes to the bathroom, Corporate Phoebe enters. She’s wearing a business suit and carrying a briefcase.

lines.text: Hey Mon!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey!

lines.stage_direction: gets up and gasps Rachel is stunned to see that her long lost friend is still fat. Monica goes over and gives Rachel a big bear hug, which is quite easy for her.

lines.text: Ohh, so do you! Did you lose weight?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh yeah.

lines.speaker: Ross

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God, sit down! Sit down! How long as it been since we’ve seen each other?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: 1987, the day after Christmas, at Sean McMahon’s party. I played you one of my songs, y’know Interplanetary Courtship Ritual.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh yeah. Right. So now, are—do you, do you still do music?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Sometimes, you should come over sometime! I’ll play you one of my other…

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God! Joey Tribbiani from Days of Our Lives , just walked in here!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Rach, he’s a friend of ours.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You are friends with Dr. Drake Remoray?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well it’s kinda hard to be friends with Drake because of his busy schedule and the fact that he’s not real.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey-hey, or I could bring my keyboard over here sometime!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: He’s coming over! He’s coming over!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Joey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I know, here-here!!
lines.stage_direction: holding a plate of what looks like Rice Crispies Treats Hands her the plate.

lines.text: Ohhh! No! This is my friend Rachel, we went to High School together.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi! I love you on that show! I watch you everyday! I mean, when you took out your own kidney to save your ex-wife even though she tired to kill you…

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, it’s always nice to meet the fans.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ah!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: She’s not crazy is she?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So uh, how you doin’?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hang on! Hang on! Hang on! Go!! Who’s this? Oh okay, you’re gonna like working for me. What’s your name? What kind of name is Brendy? I… Whatever… Stop talking! All right, from now on your name is Joan. You can pick your own last name.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey there you are!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Uh-oh, it’s my boss!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, here’s a list of things for you to do today. Man, this going to be so great! Thank you so much! All right, I got to go to work I’m delivering twins today, but only one of them is mine!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Drop off my dry cleaning. Pick up my vitamins. Teach me how to spell vitamins. Wear in my new jeans.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You realize what you are don’t you?

lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You’re his bitch.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No-no!! No!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh wait! You didn’t just sit on my Kit-Kats did you?!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No! There-there was a little, a little diff in the market and I lost 13 million dollars.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: But the Kit-Kats are all right?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What am I gonna do?! What am I gonna do?! I can’t call my office they’ll kill me! I can’t call my clients they’ll kill themselves! Great, now my chest hearts.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: My chest hurts! Oh, and now I-I can’t breathe.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Phoebe, are you having a heart attack?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, if I were, would-would I have shooting pains up and down my left arm?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Then yes that is what I’m having.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God!

lines.text: Come on Pheebs, it’s not that bad! Y’know most people would be excited if they didn’t have to work for a couple of weeks.

lines.text: Most people don’t like their jobs, I love my job! I have not been working for three hours and I’m already going crazy. I miss Joan.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Honey, having a heart attack is nature’s way of telling you to slow it down.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I always thought having a heart attack was nature’s way of telling you to die! But you’re not gonna die. I mean, you are going to die, but you’re not gonna die today. I wish I was dead.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Let’s take a walk. Y’know maybe you should consider writing for Talking Out of Your Ass magazine!

lines.text: So what’s going on with you?

lines.text: Well umm, I’ve been doing a lot more of my kara-tay.

lines.text: Yeah.

lines.text: How long has it been since you had sex?

lines.text: Well, last weekend…

lines.text: Oh that’s not so bad.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: …will be two months…

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That is.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: …since I stopped trying.

lines.text: Maybe you need to spice things up a little.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What-what do you mean?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I don’t know. You could tie her up, she could tie you up; you could eat stuff off each other…

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Y’know, dirty talk, m�nage � trois, toys…

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wow!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Roll playing… You could be the warden; she could be the prisoner. You could be the pirate; she could be the wench!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, I think I got it.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah! Or too, you could be two stockbrokers and you’re-you’re-you’re rolling around naked on the trading floor and everybody’s watching! It never happened.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Sorry. So how’s it going with Joey?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh just great. He beeps me now with codes. One is, "Bring me food." Two is, "I’m with a girl, bring us food." Three is, "I’m lost and I can’t find food."

lines.text: Hey! Is uh, is she gonna be all right?

lines.text: Yeah! She’s right in there!

lines.text: Oh great. Hey! Go take off those pants, they look ready!

lines.text: All right, and over there is Brady’s Pub where I like to unwind after a long day of surgeoning.

lines.text: Hey!

lines.text: Wow! Umm, y’know, I-I would really love to, but I-I shouldn’t.

lines.speaker: Joey

lines.text: Oh my God, I’m sorry, I’m being so rude. Rachel, would like a soda or something? Because Chandler would run right out and get it.

lines.text: Yay!

lines.text: Well I don’t know umm, what if we were too tie each other up? Umm, some people eat stuff off one another. Nah! Umm, y’know we-we could try dirty talk? Umm, we could, we could have a threesome.
lines.stage_direction: Pause Carol’s shocked and obviously doesn’t like that idea. Carol doesn’t like that idea either. Carol still says no.

lines.text: Well uh, Archie needs money to fix his jalopy , uh but he doesn’t want Reggie to just give him the money. So Reggie hires him as his assist—as his butler. And then makes him do all these crazy things like bring him milkshakes that can’t have lumps in them.

lines.text: Oh Mon, listen I have to ask! Okay, Joey Tribbiani invited me back to his apartment, now does he do this with a lot of girls?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh! And I’m one of them!! Wow! Oh, I just cannot believe this! I mean, Joey Tribbiani!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, you’re not.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, it’s so easy for you I mean, you’re not married, you get to have sex with who ever you want!

lines.speaker: Monica

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Monica. You’ve, you’ve done it right?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Of course I have! What do you think, I’m some 30 year old virgin?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God! You’re a 30 year old virgin!

lines.speaker: Monica

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It’s not like, I haven’t any opportunities. I mean, y’know, I’m just waiting for the perfect guy. I’m seeing this guy Roger, all right? He’s not perfect, but umm, I think maybe I should just get it over with. Y’know, give him my flower.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God!! Do it!! Honey, you’ve waited long enough!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Y’know what? You are right?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes!! I mean sex does not have to be a big deal! There shouldn’t be all this rules and restrictions! Y’know, people should be able to sleep with who ever they want, whenever…

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Rachel! I’m never gonna think it’s okay for you to cheat on your husband!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh what do you know? Virgin!

lines.text: Thank God.

lines.text: That’s right Wesley! I just stopped by to say that, you’re not a real doctor! And that woman’s brain, is fine!

lines.text: Hope! Hope!

lines.speaker: Hope
lines.text: Drake!

lines.text: Okay! Here we go! Okay! Hi, Joey! It’s Rachel! Umm, I am free tomorrow night. Yeah, sure, sure I can bring some sandwiches.

lines.text: Oh umm, y’know I think it would be better if we just save it.

lines.text: Okay, what is this? A stupid contest? Because we got a winner here!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I know. Yeah, his name is Pat.
lines.stage_direction: Joey is sitting in this tall chair that is made up of balls on polls. You’ll have to see it to know what I mean.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Pat the dog. Oh! Oh! I get it!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Do you uh, do you—ready for a refill?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, I probably shouldn’t—so I will! Oh! Wow! It’s like it’s raining!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Pretty cool, huh? But if you’re thinking you can put a fish in there and it wouldn’t get sucked up into the mechanism, well you’d be wrong.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Umm, can I use your bathroom?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: It’s uh, right through there.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. God y’know, if someone told me a week ago that I would be peeing in Joey Tribbiani’s apartment…

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, life’s pretty great isn’t it?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, it sure is!

scenes.scene_description: Joey's apartment, (The one he had when he was Dr. Drake Remoray, because he still is.) Rachel is there and admiring the big ceramic fake dog.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I hope you’re hungry, we’re starting with oysters. And y’know what they say about oysters, don’t you?

lines.speaker: Dr. Roger
lines.text: They have parasites?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No! Umm well, some people say that Oysters are an aphrodisiac.

lines.speaker: Dr. Roger
lines.text: What people?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: People! People say it! Come here!
lines.stage_direction: She grabs him and kisses him.

lines.speaker: Dr. Roger
lines.text: So oysters, huh?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: And then we’re gonna have a little Middle Eastern cous-cous. Something we can eat, with our hands.

lines.speaker: Dr. Roger
lines.text: Y’know, it’s funny, but when we were studying communicable diseases…

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No-no-no, no! It’s sensual!

lines.speaker: Dr. Roger
lines.text: Ohh! Didn’t know! Okay!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay! Ohhh no!
lines.stage_direction: They kiss again and his beeper goes off.

lines.speaker: Dr. Roger
lines.text: I’m sorry sweetie, it’s the hospital. The food looks great, maybe save me some?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I can’t promise anything.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: We’re really gonna do this, huh?

lines.text: Looks like it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Y’know, if, if this is too weird for you, we can still back out at…

lines.text: I got it!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: Susan! Hi!
lines.stage_direction: opening the door Who’d you think it was gonna be?

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: Thank you so much for coming.

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.text: Oh, I wouldn’t miss it for the world.

lines.speaker: Ross

lines.text: Hello Ross. I love what you’ve done with this space.
lines.stage_direction: not taking her eyes off Carol Takes off her coat and hands it to him.

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: Thank you so much.

scenes.scene_description: They start moving towards the bedroom, never taking their eyes off each other. They move past Ross and stop.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I’m sorry you’re here with me instead of Roger.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, me too.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I could make it seem like he’s here. "Here’s some little known facts about cous-cous. They didn’t add the second cous until 1979."

lines.text: Stop it!! That’s not funny!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I’m sorry, okay? It just—tonight was supposed to be y’know, it was supposed to be a big deal.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You don’t want to know what tonight was.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay. What was tonight?

lines.stage_direction: Monica shyly looks away. Monica shrugs.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right relax Mr. I’ve Had Sex Four Times!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No. He’s not a horrible guy.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey that’s what I tell girls about me.

lines.speaker: Chandler

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Chandler

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey, you’re such an amazing actor! How do you know where Dr. Drake Remoray leaves off and Joey Tribbiani begins?

lines.text: Well, with Dr. Drake they always tell me what to say. And with Joey, I pretty much have to make it up on my own.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wow! Tell me something Joey——Whoa! I just fell right off the couch there.

lines.text: Yeah you did.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wow! I can’t, I can’t feel my hands.

lines.speaker: Joey

He takes her hands in his and kisses each one, then kisses her on the lips. When the break the kiss, Rachel starts to get nauseous and throw up. Joey backs away in horror.

lines.text: We can’t do this.

lines.text: I do like that.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It’s harder for me! I have those four other women to compete with!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, if it helps there were only three. So it would just be for tonight, right?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Absolutely! It would just be one friend helping out another friend.
